title: MySQL Lock Wait Timeout on Reporting Queries
symptoms: InnoDB lock wait timeout exceeded errors across all account operations
SHOW PROCESSLIST showing 340 queries waiting for lock on accounts table
Single long-running SELECT query holding table-level lock for 15+ minutes
Application logs showing 'Lock wait timeout exceeded; try restarting transaction'
Only accounts table affected, other tables operating normally
root_cause: Long-running analytical reporting query executed against production primary instead of read replica, acquiring a lock that blocked all writes to the accounts table
resolution: Killed the blocking reporting query, waiting connections automatically retried, implemented query routing to prevent analytical queries from reaching the primary
preventive_actions: Implement query routing to automatically redirect analytical queries to read replicas
Set innodb_lock_wait_timeout to 10 seconds with application retry logic
Add long-running query detection with automatic kill after threshold
Create network-level access control limiting reporting tools to read replicas
Implement query classification and routing in the connection proxy

timeline.event: Reporting query starts executing against production primary
Table-level lock acquired on accounts table
Application writes to accounts table begin timing out
Rajesh Kumar investigates, identifies blocking query in processlist
Blocked queries automatically retry and succeed
All account operations restored, incident resolved

embedding_summary: MySQL lock wait timeouts caused by analytical reporting query accidentally run against production primary instead of read replica. Table-level lock on accounts table blocked 340 queries for 15 minutes. 800 users unable to register or update accounts. Resolved by killing blocking query and implementing query routing.
